messages.content: These risk write-ups share funnel target nodes, which does NOT make them the same risk — a funnel is coarse, so mechanistically distinct risks (a certification clock, a grid-connection queue, a distributor line-review cycle) can all compile to the same node. PARTITION the cluster into groups of TRUE duplicates: same underlying mechanism, same causal story, such that presenting both to a client would read as repetition. Distinct risks come back as singleton groups (absorbs: []). For a genuine duplicate group, pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) as keepId and fold the absorbed write-ups' UNIQUE early-warning indicators into it. Over-merging destroys distinct insights — when in doubt, keep them separate.

content: {"rationale":"The cluster contains several distinct mechanisms that happen to compile to obtainableFactor. Group 1: SOM window-semantics duplicates — 'som-window-semantics-mismatch' and 'yam-window-is-a-qualification-corridor' both argue the 3% is a multi-year benchmark misapplied to Year-1 grain, citing the same TryBuildCo/Tractian analogues and cert-corridor timing. These are the same causal story. Group 2: CRA/firmware security-audit gate — 'cra-conformity-clock' and 'firmware-audit-and-poc-attrition' both center on the security review/CRA readiness of a networked PDU as a pre-listing gate; strong mechanistic overlap. However firmware-audit-and-poc-attrition also carries a distinct POC-attrition (~30% never accepted) thread — but its core gate is the same security-audit story, so I merge and fold the attrition indicators. Group 3: German grid/EnEfG demand slip — 'grid-queue-construction-pause' and 'enefg-grid-slip-de-demand' both argue Frankfurt 24-36mo grid queues (plus NL cap / EnEfG mandates) push fit-out orders past the window; same delivery-trough mechanism on the DE/NL geographies. Group 4: pre-let lockout — 'prelet-pipeline-lockout' argues the 83% pre-let BOM is already spec'd; this is a distinct demand-availability mechanism (spec'd-out, not grid-timing), kept separate. Distinct singletons: compliance-serialization (CE/WEEE/EORI cert stack serialization — a general regulatory clock, distinct from CRA-specific and window-semantics); bundling-eliminates-bakeoffs (incumbent bundling foreclosing standalone evaluation — competitive access); distributor-line-review-lockout (annual line-review channel timing); fragmented-incumbency-price-response (incumbent price/bundle response depressing win-rate); year1-obtainable-correlated-with-slowest-cell (GTM concentration on slow large-operator cell — a structure/correlation argument). Each of these is a mechanistically distinct causal story.","groups":[{"keepId":"risk.execution-window.som-window-semantics-mismatch","absorbs":["risk.base-rate-analogy.yam-window-is-a-qualification-corridor"],"foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"Certification milestone slippage vs the launch plan (full compliance stack not complete by month 6)","where":"Notified-body/TÜV test scheduling and the venture's own conformity-assessment tracker","threshold":"Full compliance stack not complete by month 6 post-kickoff","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"POC-to-PO conversion rate and elapsed cycle time on the first five pilots","where":"Internal pipeline reviews / POC acceptance sign-offs at pilot accounts","threshold":"Median pilot-to-PO elapsed time >6 months or acceptance rate <70%","updates":"increases"}]},{"keepId":"risk.regulatory-gauntlet.cra-conformity-clock","absorbs":["risk.execution-window.firmware-audit-and-poc-attrition"],"foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"POC-stage deployment-to-acceptance attrition for industrial hardware pilots (~30% never accepted)","where":"Venture's own POC deployment and acceptance tracking; industrial-hardware pilot benchmarks","threshold":"≥30% of deployed pilots not reaching acceptance","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"RED cybersecurity delegated act enforcement dates relative to Year-1 window","where":"Official Journal of the EU / Commission standards dockets on RED 2022/30","threshold":"CRA/RED conformity dates confirmed inside the venture's Year-1 window","updates":"increases"}]},{"keepId":"risk.demand-discontinuity.grid-queue-construction-pause","absorbs":["risk.regulatory-gauntlet.enefg-grid-slip-de-demand"],"foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"EnEfG enforcement and transition-waiver practice for data centres","where":"BfEE/BAFA guidance dockets; German Datacenter Association position papers","threshold":"strict application of PUE/heat-reuse rules to 2026 commencements without transitional waivers","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Announced energization/commissioning slips at flagship German builds","where":"JLL German data-center quarterly reports; TenneT/Amprion grid-connection announcements","threshold":"≥2 flagship Frankfurt/Berlin builds announcing 6+ month slips to energization","updates":"increases"}]},{"keepId":"risk.demand-discontinuity.prelet-pipeline-lockout","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.execution-window.compliance-serialization-zeroes-h1","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.competitive-foreclosure.bundling-eliminates-bakeoffs-year1","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.competitive-foreclosure.distributor-line-review-lockout","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.base-rate-analogy.fragmented-incumbency-price-response","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.structure-independence.year1-obtainable-correlated-with-slowest-cell","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]}]}
